sourceRows.Title: The Self-Duality Equations on a Riemann Surface
sourceRows.Summary: Foundational Hitchin-equation source: a connection-plus-Higgs-field system on a Riemann surface whose moduli space became the mathematical base for Higgs-bundle geometry.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Mathematical foundation for Higgs bundles, but not an electroweak W/Z/H source law or GU-local observed-field map.

sourceRows.Title: Model Building with F-Theory
sourceRows.Summary: Uses F-theory compactifications and G-flux to derive charged chiral spectra and Yukawa couplings in N=1 compactifications.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Geometric spectrum/Yukawa lead; compactification- and flux-dependent and not a physical W/Z/H mass extraction.

sourceRows.Title: GUTs and Exceptional Branes in F-theory - I
sourceRows.Summary: Shows how local F-theory singularity geometry encodes gauge group, matter content, and Yukawa couplings through a twisted gauge theory with defects.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Strong gauge/matter/Yukawa geometry lead; no target-independent observed W/Z/H masses.

sourceRows.Title: GUTs and Exceptional Branes in F-theory - II: Experimental Predictions
sourceRows.Summary: Uses internal hypercharge flux to break a GUT group to the MSSM or flipped GUT models, with flavor and neutrino phenomenology.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Phenomenological GUT lead; hypercharge flux is not a low-energy W/Z mass source law.

sourceRows.Title: Higgs Bundles and UV Completion in F-Theory
sourceRows.Summary: Describes supersymmetric eight-dimensional Yang-Mills compactifications with meromorphic Higgs bundles and spectral covers, including a no-go theorem for generic local SU(5) three-generation models.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Closest Higgs-bundle/spectral-cover lead; still external to GU and dependent on compactification and moduli choices.

sourceRows.Title: F-theory Compactifications for Supersymmetric GUTs
sourceRows.Summary: Constructs global SU(5) F-theory GUT compactifications and states topological conditions allowing the U(1)_Y gauge boson to remain massless.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Useful photon/U(1)_Y boundary; keeping U(1)_Y massless is not an observed photon/W/Z/H projection or mass law.

sourceRows.Title: T-Branes and Monodromy
sourceRows.Summary: Introduces T-branes with non-abelian upper-triangular Higgs fields and shows spectral eigenvalues can miss localized modes.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Important warning against spectral-data-only promotion; no GU-local mass prediction.

decision: Do not promote W/Z or Higgs physical masses from Hitchin/Higgs-bundle/spectral-cover routes in this repository. These sources provide a serious connection-plus-Higgs-field and F-theory spectral-cover lead: Hitchin equations, Higgs-bundle moduli, seven-brane spectral covers, hypercharge-flux GUT breaking, and T-brane warnings about spectral data. They do not supply a GU-local Hitchin-bundle map from Shiab/observer geometry, a target-independent compactification or flux-vacuum law, observed photon/W/Z/H projection, separate W/Z source rows, Higgs scalar-source/self-coupling lineage, low-energy transport, pole extraction, or GeV normalization.
nextRequiredArtifact: A GU-local theorem deriving a Hitchin bundle or spectral-cover object from Shiab/observer-sector geometry rather than importing F-theory compactification data.
A target-independent compactification, spectral-curve, and flux-vacuum selection law yielding observed electroweak SU(2) x U(1), a massless photon, and separate W/Z source rows.
A Higgs scalar-source, doublet-projection, and self-coupling lineage from the same geometry, not from an assumed supersymmetric GUT/MSSM model.
A low-energy RG/threshold transport, physical-pole extraction, and GeV unit-normalization chain before any Hitchin/spectral-cover route can promote W/Z/H masses.
